_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Maintain Records of Processing Activities, including processing purposes, data categories, recipient categories, transfer records, retention periods, and technical and organizational security measures descriptions.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI RC domain. RC-T-006 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-K-013 (AI, cosine: 0.298), RC-K-005 (RC, cosine: 0.286).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.2/10 (raw 0.422). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [crime, process, scenes] vs WIDAI-only terms [activities, categories, data, descriptions, maintain, measures] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.874 (P=0.919, R=0.833). P exceeds R by 0.085 — the DCWF element's 3 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 23 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.9%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Process crime scenes." and "Maintain Records of Processing Activities, including processing purposes, data c". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.307 < STS 0.422 (gap -0.115). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [crime, process, scenes] and [activities, categories, data] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [crime, process, scenes]. WIDAI terms: [activities, categories, data, descriptions, maintain, measures].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 99.9% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
